title: Juan Aldama

infobox.value: (1774-01-03)January 3, 1774  San Miguél el Grande (now [San Miguel de Allende](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende")), [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato"), [New Spain](/wiki/New_Spain "New Spain")

June 26, 1811(1811-06-26) (aged 37)  [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua,_Chihuahua "Chihuahua, Chihuahua"), [Nueva Vizcaya](/wiki/Nueva_Vizcaya,_New_Spain "Nueva Vizcaya, New Spain"), New Spain (now [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico"))

(January 3, 1774 in [San Miguel el Grande](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende"), [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato") – June 26, 1811 in [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua,_Chihuahua "Chihuahua, Chihuahua")) was a Mexican revolutionary rebel soldier during the [Mexican War of Independence](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence") in 1810.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican revolutionary rebel soldier

**Juan Aldama** (January 3, 1774 in [San Miguel el Grande](/wiki/San_Miguel_de_Allende "San Miguel de Allende"), [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato") – June 26, 1811 in [Chihuahua](/wiki/Chihuahua,_Chihuahua "Chihuahua, Chihuahua")) was a Mexican revolutionary rebel soldier during the [Mexican War of Independence](/wiki/Mexican_War_of_Independence "Mexican War of Independence") in 1810.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_Aldama&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/Juan_Aldama_statue.jpg/120px-Juan_Aldama_statue.jpg)](/wiki/File:Juan_Aldama_statue.jpg)

Statue of Aldama in Chihuahua

He was also the brother of [Ignacio Aldama](/wiki/Ignacio_Aldama "Ignacio Aldama").

At the beginning of the War of Independence, Aldama was a captain of the cavalry regiment of the Queen's militia. He attended the conspiratorial meetings for independence in [Querétaro](/wiki/Quer%C3%A9taro,_Quer%C3%A9taro "Querétaro, Querétaro"), organized by [Josefa Ortiz de Domínguez](/wiki/Josefa_Ortiz_de_Dom%C3%ADnguez "Josefa Ortiz de Domínguez"), despite having to travel from San Miguel el Grande (now San Miguel de Allende) in neighboring [Guanajuato](/wiki/Guanajuato "Guanajuato").

Aldama was in San Miguel when he heard news that the conspiracy had been betrayed by a supporter who informed the Spanish colonial authorities. He traveled to [Dolores](/wiki/Dolores_Hidalgo "Dolores Hidalgo") (now Dolores Hidalgo) to inform [Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla](/wiki/Miguel_Hidalgo_y_Costilla "Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla") and [Ignacio Allende](/wiki/Ignacio_Allende "Ignacio Allende"). He witnessed the [Grito de Dolores](/wiki/Grito_de_Dolores "Grito de Dolores") ("Cry of Dolores") on the night of September 15, 1810, which started the armed conflict.

Aldama was captured by the Spanish colonial authorities on March 21, 1811 at the [Wells of Baján](/wiki/Wells_of_Baj%C3%A1n "Wells of Baján") in [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"). He was [court-martialed](/wiki/Court-martial "Court-martial") for insubordination towards the Spanish Crown and executed for [treason](/wiki/Treason "Treason"), by firing squad on June 26, 1811, together with Allende and other members of the rebel army including [Mariano Jiménez](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Mariano_Jim%C3%A9nez "José Mariano Jiménez") and [Manuel Santa María](/w/index.php?title=Manuel_Santa_Mar%C3%ADa&action=edit&redlink=1 "Manuel Santa María (page does not exist)").

Aldama's body was decapitated and his head taken to the [Alhóndiga de Granaditas](/wiki/Alh%C3%B3ndiga_de_Granaditas "Alhóndiga de Granaditas") where it was shown to the public inside a cage hung from one corner of the building. In 1824, his remains were moved to an [altar](/wiki/Altar "Altar") in the [Metropolitan Cathedral](/wiki/Metropolitan_Cathedral,_Mexico_City "Metropolitan Cathedral, Mexico City") in [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"). Finally, in 1925, his remains were moved one last time to the mausoleum in the [Independence Column](/wiki/El_%C3%81ngel "El Ángel") in Mexico City.

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Juan_Aldama&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Legacy")]

Aldama is a national [hero](/wiki/Hero "Hero") of Mexico. In his honor, several towns and villages are named after him. The following category includes:

* The municipality of [Juan Aldama, Zacatecas](/wiki/Municipality_of_Juan_Aldama "Municipality of Juan Aldama").
* The municipality of [Villaldama, Nuevo León](/wiki/Villaldama,_Nuevo_Le%C3%B3n "Villaldama, Nuevo León").
* The municipality of [Aldama, Tamaulipas](/wiki/Aldama,_Tamaulipas "Aldama, Tamaulipas").
* The municipality of [Aldama, Chihuahua](/wiki/Aldama,_Chihuahua "Aldama, Chihuahua").
* The municipality of [Villa Aldama](/wiki/Villa_Aldama "Villa Aldama"), Veracruz
* The city of [León, Guanajuato](/wiki/Le%C3%B3n,_Guanajuato "León, Guanajuato"), was officially renamed to "León de los Aldama" in honor of brothers Juan and Ignacio Aldama.
* The municipality of [Los Aldamas, Nuevo León](/w/index.php?title=Los_Aldamas,_Nuevo_Le%C3%B3n&action=edit&redlink=1 "Los Aldamas, Nuevo León (page does not exist)") was also named after him and his brother.
* The village of Juan Aldama, "El Tigre", Sinaloa outside of [Culiacán](/wiki/Culiac%C3%A1n "Culiacán").
* The street Juan Aldama in [Mexicali](/wiki/Mexicali "Mexicali"), [Baja California](/wiki/Baja_California "Baja California") in the Colonia Independencia (neighborhood).
